message:
feat(frankenpandas): expose CSV/Excel write_*_with_options surface (fd90.216 / br-jy9vq)

Five fp-io exports were completely hidden from the frankenpandas
crate — not reachable via either prelude or top-level path:

- CsvWriteOptions (delimiter, na_rep, header, include_index,
  index_label) — matches pandas to_csv kwargs
- ExcelWriteOptions (sheet_name, index, index_label, header) —
  matches pandas to_excel kwargs
- write_csv_string_with_options
- write_excel_with_options (path-based)
- write_excel_bytes_with_options (in-memory)

The read/write surface was asymmetric: CsvReadOptions, ExcelReadOptions,
read_csv_with_options, read_csv_with_options_path, etc. were all in the
prelude (per fd90.207, fd90.215). The write side had nothing equivalent.

Fix:
- Add the 5 items to the top-level fp_io re-export block
- Add the same 5 to the prelude module
- Extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard with type-checks for
  CsvWriteOptions::default(), ExcelWriteOptions::default(), and
  fn-pointer name-checks for the three writers

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -540,6 +540,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-jpr","title":"Add fill propagation metamorphic properties","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-17T09:07:45.688667566Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T09:28:15.286657048Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T09:28:15.284910162Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-jxwwk","title":"fd90.110: README scores.ge(&Scalar) wrong — Series::ge takes &Series; should be ge_scalar","description":"fd90.74-109 README audit. Lines 1070-1071 in case_when example show:

  let graded = scores.case_when(&[
      (scores.ge(&Scalar::Int64(90))?, Series::constant("A", n)?),
      (scores.ge(&Scalar::Int64(80))?, Series::constant("B", n)?),
  ])?;

scores.ge() expects another Series, not a Scalar:
  crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:2222 — pub fn ge(&self, other: &Self) -> Result<Self, FrameError>
  crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:2303 — pub fn ge_scalar(&self, scalar: &Scalar) -> Result<Self, FrameError>

The Scalar→Series comparison is ge_scalar. A user copy-pasting would hit:
  error[E0308]: mismatched types — expected reference '&Series', found reference '&Scalar'

Fix: ge → ge_scalar on both lines (lines 1070 and 1071) since the right operands are Scalars. Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:38:41.392390802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:40:30.535435985Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:40:30.532702309Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-jy6q","title":"fp-io read_csv skipfooter parameter parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T05:42:04.781969223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T05:49:09.631846008Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T05:49:09.629987690Z","close_reason":"Added skipfooter field + truncation after nrows, 4 unit tests, clippy clean","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-jy9vq","title":"fd90.216: Expose CsvWriteOptions/ExcelWriteOptions + 3 write_*_with_options to prelude","description":"fd90.216: Expose CSV/Excel write_*_with_options surface (5 items) at top-level + prelude.

Five fp-io exports are completely hidden from the frankenpandas crate:

- CsvWriteOptions (struct: delimiter, na_rep, header, include_index, index_label)
- ExcelWriteOptions (struct: sheet_name, index, index_label, header)
- write_csv_string_with_options (fn taking &CsvWriteOptions)
- write_excel_with_options (fn taking &ExcelWriteOptions, path-based)
- write_excel_bytes_with_options (fn taking &ExcelWriteOptions, in-memory)

The README mentions CSV options "delimiter, headers, na_values, index_col, usecols, nrows, skiprows, dtype" (line 141) and Excel options "sheet_name, has_headers, index_col, skip_rows" (line 145). Read paths have CsvReadOptions and ExcelReadOptions in the prelude. Write paths have nothing.

User code that wants to call write_csv_string_with_options(&df, &CsvWriteOptions { delimiter: b'\t', ... }) cannot reach either type or the function from the prelude or even the top-level frankenpandas module.

Scope:
- Add CsvWriteOptions, ExcelWriteOptions, write_csv_string_with_options, write_excel_with_options, write_excel_bytes_with_options to the top-level re-exports (lib.rs:58-154 block)
- Add the same 5 to the prelude
- Extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard

Files: crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs.
Time estimate: 20-30min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:40:25.374236233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:42:29.850941683Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T22:42:29.848929051Z","close_reason":"Exposed CsvWriteOptions, ExcelWriteOptions, write_csv_string_with_options, write_excel_with_options, write_excel_bytes_with_options at both the top-level frankenpandas re-exports AND the prelude module. All five existed in fp_io but were entirely hidden from the frankenpandas crate (not even reachable via use frankenpandas::CsvWriteOptions). With this commit, the read/write surface is symmetric — read paths have CsvReadOptions/ExcelReadOptions + read_csv_with_options/read_csv_with_options_path/read_excel*, write paths now have CsvWriteOptions/ExcelWriteOptions + write_csv_string_with_options/write_excel_with_options/write_excel_bytes_with_options. Extended prelude_completeness_compile_guard with type-checks. All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-k05t","title":"[HIGH] conformance: every fixture is sub-4KB — pandas parity untested on any frame size > ~30 rows","description":"Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses skill's Coverage Accounting Matrix rule to frame size distribution.

**Observation.** find + stat on crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/*.json:
  count=1249  min=186  median=969  p95=1702  max=3237  mean=992  (bytes)

Every fixture is sub-4KB. The largest fixture is 3237 bytes — ~30 rows × a handful of columns at most. Zero fixtures exercise:
- Large-frame arithmetic (10k / 100k / 1M rows)
- Group cardinality > ~10 (pandas GroupBy has distinct fast paths at different cardinalities: dense Int64 keys, range-index, small-set hash, large-cardinality hash — our impl mirrors this per README's "three automatic execution paths")
- Column-count > ~10 (arrow batch-boundary handling; pandas stress tests at 100+ cols)
- Long string values (Utf8 column of 4096-byte strings; pandas has 2GB string-array limit, ours has rust-String heap limit)
- Wide-index scenarios (frame with 50+ levels of row MultiIndex for the 1zzp.5 conformance; currently row-MI fixtures are 2-level max)

**Consequence.** The conformance claim says pandas parity, but only on toy inputs. The exact bug classes that manifest at scale — int overflow on cumulative sum, SIMD boundary conditions, group-aggregation fast-path vs slow-path divergence, memory-layout edge cases in arrow/parquet round-trips — have zero conformance coverage. The AG-04/AG-05 claim in README (87% groupby speedup via three auto execution paths) is untested against pandas on any frame big enough to hit the non-dense path.

**Skill rule violated:** Coverage Accounting Matrix requires enumerating EVERY testable requirement. "Behavior on a 1M-row frame equals pandas' behavior on the same 1M-row frame" is a requirement. Not-tested = not-covered = not-conformant.

**Severity.** HIGH — the product's performance claims are at the scale where bugs would actually matter for users; conformance is tested at the scale where performance is irrelevant. The gap between "tested" and "claimed" is three orders of magnitude.

**Fix shape:**

Tier structure (balances CI runtime vs coverage):
1. **Tier-S fixtures** (ship today): small (≤ 30 rows, ≤ 5 cols). Current 1249 fixtures all fall here. Runs every PR.
2. **Tier-M fixtures** (new): medium (1k-10k rows, 5-20 cols). Runs every PR but behind `fp-conformance-medium` feature or `--test-threads=1`. Expected: 50-100 new fixtures covering each fast-path-triggering size threshold for groupby, rolling, arithmetic.
3. **Tier-L fixtures** (new, nightly only): large (100k+ rows). Runs on scheduled nightly CI. Expected: 20-30 new fixtures covering the scale-specific divergence classes.

Generator sketch:
- `oracle/generate_scale_fixtures.py` produces parametric fixtures: for each (size_tier, op_class) pair, generate 3-5 fixtures at representative sizes. Deterministic seed so fixtures are reproducible.
- `crates/fp-conformance/tests/scale_conformance.rs` loads tier-M and tier-L fixtures and runs them through the same assert_*_expected comparators.

CI integration:
- Existing `cargo test -p fp-conformance` runs Tier-S (status quo).
- New `cargo test -p fp-conformance --features scale-m` runs Tier-S + Tier-M. Wired to PR CI.
- New scheduled nightly workflow runs Tier-S + Tier-M + Tier-L and uploads timing metrics.

Blast radius: +1 Python generator, +1 Rust test file, +50-130 fixtures (but each tiny-to-medium size — total disk growth ~5-10 MB), +1 CI workflow for nightly scale. No changes to existing fixtures.

Related: br-ing6 (perf regression gate) — scale fixtures complement it by checking correctness-at-scale, while ing6 checks throughput-at-scale. Both needed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:10:41.578998944Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:29:38.638578Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:29:38.638578Z","close_reason":"Phase 1 scaffolding: generator + scale_conformance.rs harness + FP_SCALE_{M,L} gate. Fixture population deferred.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","fixtures","review","scale"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-k1b4n","title":"fd90.120: README df.melt(... "quarter", "sales") wants Option<&str> for var_name/value_name","description":"fd90.74-119 README audit. Line 507 in Reshaping section shows:

  let melted = df.melt(&["id"], &["q1", "q2", "q3"], "quarter", "sales")?;

But crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:18925 declares:
  pub fn melt(&self, id_vars: &[&str], value_vars: &[&str], var_name: Option<&str>, value_name: Option<&str>) -> Result<Self, FrameError>

The 3rd and 4th args are Option<&str>, not &str. The README passes raw string literals 'quarter' and 'sales'. A user copy-pasting hits:
  error[E0308]: expected enum 'Option', found '&str'

Fix: wrap in Some(...): df.melt(&["id"], &["q1","q2","q3"], Some("quarter"), Some("sales"))?. Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:17:37.083478127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:18:47.439352856Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:18:47.436646850Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
